other_observers: none other than my 5-year old son Garrett but I don't know how much detail he noted on the bird...; Connie Kogler
description: I arrived at the Tunnel Campground where Connie Kogler found & photographed a female Magnificent Hummingbird a few days before (& the summer before.) As I was driving in (afternoon- 4:30-ish) I noticed a lot of activity at the host's three feeders. I stopped the car and watched for a few minutes when a monster greenish-backed hummer with a long, fairly decurved bill and strong facial pattern arrived to feed. It was substantially larger than the mostly Broad-tailed Hummers and scattering of Rufous that were around and I had no doubt it was the Mag. I set up camp, put up a feeder, and got my camera ready but didn't see the bird again for the following two days of my visit. It didn't strike me as dark as a Blue-throated (either underparts or tail) and I think the bill was too long and curved for Blue-throated as well.Behaviors: Using hummingbird feeders.Call: Not heard.Plumage: Adult
